comments.author: @flying_787

comments.text:
You will get this device with the ICICI Lombard policy only if 
1. you include RSA in your policy
AND
2. only if your car's age is upto 10 years....policy document does not mention this clearly but they remove some features from RSA (as your car crosses the 10 year mark) which are mandatory for your policy to be eligible for this device
comments.author: @abhisheksalvi9189

comments.text: Thanks for sharing additional information - was not aware of the RSA being a mandatory add-on
comments.author: @SakkathGadgets

comments.text: Thanks for the review. Does it cause battery drain? If the device is not connected during an accident, will icici reject the claim?
comments.author: @nagendranr24

comments.text: Here is the official statement from IL Assist: "The battery consumption is 70mA/hour. When the car ignition is turned off, the device goes in the sleep mode within 5-10 minutes and the battery consumption while in the sleep mode is very less, about 10mA. The device is not capable to writing anything back to the car, hence it doesn't interfere with the car performance. It is a read-only device, and simply communicates all the car related activities to you via the mobile app IL Assist."
comments.author: @SakkathGadgets

Also, I did make a claim with ICICI and it was a good experience but that was 2+ years back - so cannot comment on Claim scenario more recently.
